tests_point: when the agent designs the signature itself, with NO prompt hint about failure, a value that can be missing lands in the type (String? or raise) instead of a bare String with an "" sentinel — mirrors a real-world git_output() -> String that returned "" on process failure
naive_failure: current_branch(repo) -> String returning "" (or stdout.trim() unconditionally) when the command fails
verify: .mbti: current_branch returns String? or raises; bare String fails the design gate
behavioral conformance matched to the chosen shape (healthy repos report the branch; broken repo yields None / raises)
notes: Result[...] return shapes fail the gate too (un-idiomatic per errors.md); prompt deliberately omits any absence/failure requirement — the delta measured is design propensity, so companion behavioral task absence-not-empty-sentinel covers the spec-following axis.
